texto: The only thing "free" about so-called free time is that it doesn't cost the boss anything. Free time is mostly devoted to getting ready for work, going to work, returning from work, and recovering from work. Free time is an euphemism for the peculiar way labor as a factor of production not only transports itself at its own expense to and from the workplace but assumes primary responsibility for its own maintenance and repair.
explicacao: Citação de Bob Black sobre passagem do tempo e prioridades. “The only thing "free" about so-called free time is that it doesn't cost the boss anything. Free t…” articula trabalho, capitalism, freedom em registro de trabalho, com relevância para contextos como trabalho e freedom.

frase_original: The only thing "free" about so-called free time is that it doesn't cost the boss anything. Free time is mostly devoted to getting ready for work, going to work, returning from work, and recovering from work. Free time is an euphemism for the peculiar way labor as a factor of production not only transports itself at its own expense to and from the workplace but assumes primary responsibility for its own maintenance and repair.

texto: Work, then, institutionalizes homicide as a way of life. People think the Cambodians were crazy for exterminating themselves, but are we any different? The Pol Pot regime at least had a vision, however blurred, of an egalitarian society. We kill people in the six-figure range (at least) in order to sell Big Macs and Cadillacs to the survivors. Our forty or fifty thousand annual highway fatalities are victims, not martyrs. They died for nothing — or rather, they died for work. But work is nothing to die for.
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Bob Black que provoca sentido da existência e experiência vivida. Ao questionar “Work, then, institutionalizes homicide as a way of life. People think the Cambodians were crazy f…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre reflexao, anarchism, capitalism, com foco em trabalho e capitalism.

texto: You are what you do. If you do boring, stupid monotonous work, chances are you'll end up boring, stupid and monotonous. Work is a much better explanation for the creeping cretinization all around us than even such significant moronizing mechanisms as television and education.
explicacao: Citação de Bob Black sobre temas ligados a educacao. “You are what you do. If you do boring, stupid monotonous work, chances are you'll end up boring, …” articula educacao, anarchism, neo-situationism em registro de educacao, com relevância para contextos como termino e trabalho.
